title: $keyfields
text: $keyfields Return a list of the fields that make up a primary or candidate key or index. $keyfields ( Entity , KeyNumber ) Example: vList = $keyfields(CUSTOMER, 2) Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Entity String Entity name. KeyNumber Numeric Key identifier; the value will be truncated to form an integer. 1, the primary key. 2, 3, 4, and so on, the number that identifies a candidate key that has been defined for Entity . Return Values String that contains an indexed list of the fields that make up the specified key or index. An empty string ("") is returned if the referenced entity is a non-database entity, or if an error occurred. $procerror contains a negative value that identifies the exact error. Table 2. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following this Function Value Error constant Meaning -1102 <UPROCERR_ENTITY> The entity name provided is not a valid name or the entity is not present in the component structure. -1104 <UPROCERR_KEY> The key number provided is not valid; for example, the key number was out of range. Use Allowed in all component types, except self-contained Reports. Example: Using $keyfields vMaxKeys = $totkeys vKeyNum = 1 while (vKeyNum <= vMaxKeys ) putmess "Keyfields = %%$keyfields($entname, vKeyNum)%%%" vKeyNum = vKeyNum + 1 endwhile Copy Related Links Validate Determines whether the valerr data processing attribute is added for the selected entity node in the DTD tree. This attribute holds the occurrence or field validation error (depending on whether the selected item is an entity or field). Valid values are True or False (default). validatefield Validate a field of the current occurrence. validatekey Validate a key of the current occurrence. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
